All right, you're back. Notice I am at my desk. Bookcase behind me. Different shirt. I want you to change your state here. Notice I'm talking more slowly. Not the excited Jordan here. It's just the mellow Jordan, right? There's a reason for that. The exercise we're going to do now is going to be one way you're in a very comfortable, relaxed state. Remember it's not hypnosis. You're going to be in complete control the whole time. I'm going to take you on a little trip here down memory lane, so to speak, by having you draw your timeline out. I'm going to take you through that process step by step, very slowly, very deliberately. So please make sure that you're in a quiet place, about 20 minutes long this exercise. And I can promise you that when it's done, if you really play full out here, and really just really insinuate into this exercise here and do it seriously, the impact is going to be profound. It's going to be immediate. And your life will change for the better in a dramatic way as a result of that. Okay? A couple of quick points here. Number one, I want to thank Dr. Banler again for inventing this program, but not just that, but teaching it to me personally. There's a very big difference with NLP when you get taught by someone who just kind of knows it versus hearing it from the horse's mouth. And because of Dr. Banler, I can really use this stuff at an incredibly high level and empower you. All right? Lastly one more point here, and see I'm speaking very slowly. I want you to kind of relax, right? Last and final point is just remember that your unconscious mind is always in control. You're not being hypnotized. If at any moment you want out, you open up your eyes and you stop, but I'm not asking you to do anything overly painful. There'll be some moments where you'll feel a bit uncomfortable because we're looking back at what are negative experiences that caused your limiting belief. But the way we're going to do this is we're going to have you actually put yourself inside a helicopter bubble. This is one of Banler's great tricks. And float up. You'll float up and be above your own timeline. And that means you're in a dissociated state, meaning you're looking down at your old self. And by doing that, it essentially takes out the bulk of the emotion. In other words, you can look at it logically. You can experience the emotions you want, but because you're above it and not actually in it, the impact that has to be far less. So you're safe. Now, once we do that, the next step is I'll be doing what's called a safety sandwich and create a safety sandwich for you. Meaning I give you a moment before the limiting belief was first inserted in you, right? Because that's how it happens. You experience something, it's inserted in you. So I'm going to take you right before and then a few minutes after. So you have a safe zone either side before the experience and then after the experience ended and you were feeling safe again, but now you had this limiting belief. So you always have these two buffers on either side, point being, there's nothing to worry about. All right? With that, let's get into this exercise right now. And you'll notice a change in my voice is probably a bit deeper because I filmed this. It was an audio very high resolution sound in a studio. It's very deep, almost baritone. That's for a reason because it will resonate with your unconscious mind more deeply. And about 18 minutes, 17 minutes later, I'll see you on the other side. We'll have a quick conversation and then we'll move on. Okay? Enjoy this, relax and I'll see you soon. Now I want you to sit in a comfortable position, feet flat on the floor, hands in your lap and take a deep breath in and let it out slowly. Now do it again and let your eyes gently close. Now you're going to take a little trip here. Your unconscious mind may or may not be involved. Let everything go and know that your unconscious mind will keep you safe and only wants the best for you. So you can totally, totally relax. Now as you're sitting in your chair, noticing how your feet feel as they touch the floor, your hands feel as they gently rest on your lap, your back resting against the chair, the feel of the air on your skin and the sound of my voice all helping you be very, very comfortable. Make any small adjustments that you may need in order to be as comfortable as possible and to relax more deeply now. And as you sit in the chair, get a sense of where you are in this moment, sitting here in this room with your eyes closed right now. In a moment, I'm going to ask you to point to a place where you would feel that the present moment is for you. If you had to say where you see the present moment right now, where would it be? Some people it's in front of them. For others it's inside them or beside them or even behind them. Once you have a sense of where the present moment is for you, point to it now. That's right. Now think of a moment from today. Maybe you're getting ready to come to work. Maybe you're waking up and brushing your teeth or getting dressed or driving to work. Remember one moment from earlier today. If you had to point to where that moment was, where it occurred, where would it be? Get a sense of exactly where that moment shows up for you. Is it behind you? Is it beside you, to your left, to your right? Is it lower or higher? Point to exactly where that memory of this morning is for you. And again, there's no right or wrong answer to whatever you feel that moment is for you. Now I want you to remember something pleasant happened maybe a year ago. Perhaps a birthday or a trip that you had with your family or someone that you loved and cared about or a moment of great success or happiness. I want you to see and feel that memory as clearly as you possibly can. Where were you? What were you doing? Who else was there with you? What did you see? Notice if your eyes are looking in a particular direction as you remember that moment. Where does that memory show up for you? When I say so, point to the place where that memory appears for you. On the count of three, one, two, three, point to that memory of a year ago. Now see your timing of the past extending from where you are now through the point you saw this morning, through the point you saw the memory of a year ago. Now put your hand down and come back to the present moment. Fill yourself back in the chair sitting comfortably. And as you sit there, imagine yourself a year from now in the future. Where will you be? What will you be doing? What will you be seeing, hearing, feeling? Notice how your eyes move to a particular spot as you visualize your future a year from now. When I say so, point to the place where that vision of the future is for you. One, two, three, point to the future. That's right. Now see your future timeline extending from your present from where you are right now through your vision of a year from now into your future. With your eyes closed now, use your finger to trace the line from the present moment to a year from now, then off into your distant future. That's right. Now use your finger to trace your entire timeline from the present back to the past, then back to the present, and then off into the future. Now back to the present, then back into your past, to the present, and then to the future. That's right. That's your timeline. Now I want you to imagine that you're floating up above your body, about six, eight feet above your body. Now you're inside one of those glass helicopter bubbles. And notice that inside the bubble, there are a couple of buttons. These are your controls. One of them is a stick, like a joystick for moving this way or that way, like in a video game. You can go left, right, forward, back. And the more force that you put on the joystick, the faster you'd move. I want you to just take this hand. I'll have a little fun with it for a second, and just cruise around the room that you're in right now using this joystick. And just know that you can take this thing wherever you want. You're the best pilot in the world. Just cruise around for a minute or so, 30 seconds is enough. Just cruise around your little helicopter bubble, this glass bubble, and just cruise outside now around that city or town that you're in. You can go outdoors for a bit. Just have a bit of fun here. You can just jet right out, and you can go as fast as you possibly want, and cruise all the way around. And now what I want you to do is this. In your helicopter bubble, you're safe. You're completely safe in this bubble. I want you to turn around so you're facing your past. And now slowly, I want you to push forward on your joystick and start heading back into the past. And they move up a bit now, perhaps 12 to 15 feet above your timeline, so you're looking downward at your past. Now notice how you see all the events of your life as points along that timeline, moments and events that shape you both for good and for ill, haven't they? And remember that you were above your timeline experiencing these moments from a distance. You're not actually in them, so you're not really feeling them viscerally. You can see things almost objectively. And as you're floating above your past timeline, I want you to remember that old, negative, limiting belief that you wrote down. The belief that you said you want to get rid of, the one that had an experience attached to it. Something that happened that caused you to adopt that belief as being true. Note along the timeline into the past and see the events of your life passing underneath you until you come upon the earliest moment that you can remember when you first had that limiting belief. And I want you to float back to that earliest memory you have, an early experience that might have formed the actual root of this limiting belief. This is called your root experience. And when you get there to this root experience, I want you to hover over it. It might be a dark spot, not overly dark, but it might very well be a dark spot. I want you to float over that limiting belief and just hover right there. And as you look down at that belief, that experience, notice that inside the helicopter you have some other buttons as well. One button is for certainty. Another is for knowledge. Another is for unconditional love. And at any moment that you want, right now, you can push any one of these buttons and give yourself that resource. It will just simply wash over you and you will be a watch in that resource. Sometimes we float backwards and we think that we've found the root experience, but in fact we haven't because when we get there and look at it, it wakes something else up inside us. And then we realize that there's actually something that happened even before this. So take another deep breath right now and I want you to float backwards again and see if you can find an even earlier experience. If you can't, then just float back to this one. But if you could float back further, when I count the 10, if you're not sure where you're going, your unconscious mind will automatically put you exactly where you need to be. Remember your unconscious mind is much more powerful. So even if you can't remember yourself consciously, your unconscious mind remembers exactly where this root experience is. So when I count the 10, you're going to stop right there hovering right above that first root experience that caused you to have this limiting belief. One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten. You're there. Your unconscious mind always knows. Now what we're going to do is we're going to actually form what's called the safety sandwich. So we're going to go back two or three minutes, maybe even five minutes before this experience and when you get there, I want you to stop and put a little marker there. And make it a white marker so you know that it's before this belief ever happened. And notice that any feelings that you may have had are gone now because this experience hasn't even happened yet. And the reason you'll put a little marker there is so we can get back there if you ever need to. Now I want you to float up a little bit higher, maybe 12 feet above the line and cruise forward slowly looking at this experience, not drawing any meanings to it right now, but I want you to float forward until about three minutes after this experience. Now it could be even further, maybe even a week after this experience, whatever time it takes you when you no longer feel the effects of that experience that first caused this limiting belief. So yes, you have the belief now because you've experienced it, but you're not feeling uncomfortable. You feel safe again. It was a negative experience. It's over. If you need to go, you know, again, five minutes later, 15 minutes later, a week later, that's fine too. But I want you to have a safe place to go on both sides of the experience before and after. And when you get to that spot after the experience and you're comfortable, put down a white marker there too, a little white line, thick white line. And notice that you have these two lines now and turn around so you're now facing the past. I want you to float up to your first white line before the experience happened now. I want you to turn around forward in your helicopter bubble so you're now facing towards the present. And I want you to understand that right now, while we don't have the power to change our memories, we certainly have the power to change the meanings that we draw from them. And before we move forward and relive this experience, I want you to push any of the buttons that you think you need. Confidence, clarity, compassion, courage, faith, love. It doesn't matter what it is, compassion, anything you need, whatever resource you need, push the button and notice how you become a wash in this resource. It just seems to flow all over you, every pore of your body. Now notice inside this bubble is a button very far on the right. I want you to press that button now and notice that the bottom door hatch, a little hatch opens up and you could actually exit the helicopter and float down to your timeline. Press that button now. I want you to descend outside the helicopter and float down with all the resources that you need. Go right down to that little white line where you're safe. You're floating in the air just a foot or two above the line right there in the scene and I want you to relive this experience again. See what people were doing. Hear what they were saying.
